excerpt: Every messenger stands within the legal tradition of his predecessor, drawing upon commandments already established before him, emerging from the community of believers, refined through purification of self, knowledge, and capacity, until recognized and established in prophetic rank by the Imam of the preceding age.
source_authority: High confidence (Ismaili theological systematic doctrine on prophethood and succession)
source_location_hint: Doctrine of the prophetic station and sharīʿa-bearing (ṣāḥib al-sharīʿa) succession; standard Ismaili theological exposition

excerpt: Allah created all creation for the purpose of the Essence of the World—Muḥammad al-Muṣṭafā—which stands as the greatest blessing and mercy to mankind. This Essence required preliminary stages for its manifestation; for this reason, He created Adam and all the other prophets, each an instrument in the cosmic unfolding toward this supreme purpose.
source_authority: High confidence (foundational Ismaili cosmology and prophetic purpose)
source_location_hint: Doctrine of cosmic purpose and prophetic preparation; teleological cosmology centered on Muḥammad

excerpt: It is transmitted that Allah revealed to Jesus: 'O Jesus, believe in Muḥammad and command your people to believe in him.' He said: 'Had Muḥammad not been destined to appear, I would not have created Adam, nor Paradise, nor Hell. When I established My Throne upon water, it trembled. When I inscribed upon it the shahāda, it became fixed. The Throne rests upon water, which symbolizes knowledge, showing that knowledge is indispensable for attainment of Allah and true monotheism.'
source_authority: High confidence (Qur'anic taʾwīl and cosmological narrative; Ismaili ta'lim material)
source_location_hint: Prophetic pre-eternity narratives and cosmological symbolism; Imamic teachings

excerpt: The Prophet said to Abū Dharr al-Ghifārī: 'O Abū Dharr, be as a traveler in this world. Consider yourself as one who is dead. Speak little; remember Allah much. Through this you will conquer Shayṭān. Speak truth even if the listener finds it bitter. Love the poor and sit in their gatherings. Be content with a morsel of bread and a garment, and find happiness in this. Love not the world, for it is the root of every sin.'
source_authority: High confidence (authenticated prophetic ḥadīth material in Ismaili jurisprudence)
source_location_hint: Chapter on asceticism and avoidance of worldly desires; ḥadīth of moral counsel

excerpt: There are two forms of worship of Shayṭān: one is the open disobedience of Allah's commands; the other is outward obedience to His commands, yet performed not as Allah has revealed but through innovation and the alteration of religion. Some pray, yet they innovate prayers like the night vigil (Tarāwīḥ), which is a bid'a.
source_authority: High confidence (Ismaili doctrine on true worship and rejection of innovation)
source_location_hint: Discourse on authentic versus counterfeit obedience; critique of unauthorized religious practices

excerpt: Sins committed with wholehearted pleasure and tongue-praise can be forgiven only through sincere repentance and remorse—there is no other path. Such a person must turn earnestly to Allah, begging forgiveness for his transgressions, and must restrain his tongue from lauding evil.
source_authority: High confidence (standard Islamic and Ismaili jurisprudence on repentance and forgiveness)
source_location_hint: Doctrine of tawba (repentance) and moral reformation; esoteric exegesis of sin and atonement

excerpt: The Prophet said: 'I am near to the broken-hearted.' From this can be understood that the slip-ups and errors of the prophets were of this character: as soon as they sought forgiveness, their ranks were elevated, and they drew ever closer to Allah.
source_authority: High confidence (authenticated prophetic and Imamic ḥadīth; foundational prayer tradition)
source_location_hint: Spiritual teachings on divine proximity and the broken heart; supplication and spiritual states

excerpt: Because the Holy Prophet's personality embodies the light in which all former radiances converge and all previous prophetic attributes merge and unify, his characteristics and qualities necessarily comprise the sum of all theirs. History repeats itself; those trials, disagreements, and calamities that appeared in ages past appeared likewise in the time of Muḥammad al-Muṣṭafā.
source_authority: High confidence (foundational Shīʿī theological and prophetic doctrine)
source_location_hint: Doctrine of prophetic inheritance and comprehensiveness; sermons on the seal of the prophets
